questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which from following polymers is used to obtain tyre cords?
questions.solution: Due to its high tensile strength and luster, nylon 6 fibres are used for manufacturing of tyre cords.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following will form a biodegradable polymer?
questions.solution: Glycine and aminocaproic acid forms nylon- 2 -nylon- 6 , which is a biodegradable polymer.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which among the following statements is NOT true for $\mathrm{LDP}$ ?
options: It needs $\mathrm{O}_2$ or peroxide as initiator synthesis process.
It is a branched chain polymer.
It needs low pressure about $6-7$ atm in synthesis process.
It has low melting point than HDP.
questions.solution:
LDP is prepared by polymerization of ethylene under high pressure $(1000-2000 \mathrm{~atm})$ and temperature
$(350-570 \mathrm{~K})$ in presence of traces of $\mathrm{O}_2$ or peroxide as initiator.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which from following polymers is obtained from isoprene?
questions.solution: Natural rubber is a high molecular mass linear polymer of isoprene (2-methylbuta-1,3-diene).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following set of polymers are used as fibre?
(i) Teflon
(ii) Starch
(iii) Terylene
(iv) Orlon
questions.solution: Among the given sets of polymer terylene and orlon are used as fibres. Fibres are the thread forming solids which possess high tensile strength and high modulus. These characteristics is due to strong intermolecular forces like hydrogen bonds.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The biodegradable polymer obtained by polymerisation of glycine and aminocaproic acid is
questions.solution:
The biodegradable polymer
Nylon-2, nylon 6, is an alternating polyamide copolymer of glycine $\left[\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{COOH}\right]$ and amino caproic acid $\left[\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N}\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_5 \mathrm{COOH}\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which one is chain growth polymer?
questions.solution:
Chain growth polymers are formed by the chain growth polymerisation or chain polymerisation. This polymerisation process involves in a series of reaction each of which consumes a relative particles and produces another similar particle resulting a chain reaction. Teflon is a chain growth polymer. It is the polymer of tetraflouro ethylene.
$\left.\underset{\text { Tetrofluoro ethylene }}{n\left(\mathrm{CF}_2\right.}=\mathrm{CF}_2\right) \xrightarrow[\text { Pressure }]{\text { High }}-\left(\mathrm{CF}_2 \underset{\text { Teflon }}{\left.-\mathrm{CF}_2\right)_n}\right.$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Natural rubber is a polymer of monomer isoprene. During polymerisaton
options: both double bonds are converted into single bond
questions.solution: Natural rubber is a linear polymers of isoprene and is also called as cis-1, 4-polyisoprene. It is a linear 1, 4-polymer of isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
